speedZones.name: Touch
speedZones.description: Soft, touch putts for short distances

speedZones.name: Power
speedZones.description: Powerful putts for the longest greens

tracks.title: First Speed — 3 MPH
tracks.objective: Establish the kinesthetic anchor for 3 MPH — the softest putt in the program. Today is about exploration and discovery, not perfection.

blocks.name: Free Exploration
blocks.description: Hit putts at whatever feels natural. The app is gathering its first data on your stroke.

blocks.name: Anchoring 3 MPH
blocks.description: 3 MPH is the softest putt in the program — think of a 2–3 foot putt on a fast green. Minimal backswing, smooth acceleration, quiet hands.

tracks.coachingNotes: Expect inconsistency. The brain is gathering data. Trust the process.

tracks.title: Adding 4 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce 4 MPH and begin discriminating 3 vs 4. Heavy blocked practice anchors the new speed; cumulative work keeps yesterday's speed alive.

blocks.name: Warmup
blocks.description: Ease in with the speeds you know — adaptive engine emphasizes whichever speed you're weaker on.

blocks.name: Anchoring 4 MPH
blocks.description: 4 MPH is one notch firmer than 3. Same stroke shape, slightly more pace.

blocks.name: Alternating 3 and 4
blocks.description: Switch between 3 and 4. Notice your brain auto-correcting in real time.

blocks.name: Cumulative — Learned Pool
blocks.description: Random rotation of every speed learned so far, weighted toward your weaker one.

tracks.coachingNotes: Notice the small physical difference between 3 and 4. Both speeds should feel distinct by the end.

tracks.title: Adding 5 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce 5 MPH. Three speeds in rotation begins building the discrimination schema.

blocks.name: Warmup
blocks.description: Adaptive warmup across previously learned speeds (3, 4), sorted slow → fast.

blocks.name: Anchoring 5 MPH
blocks.description: 5 MPH is a 6-foot lag putt. Establish it as a distinct, repeatable feel.

blocks.name: Sequence 3-4-5
blocks.description: Three speeds in ascending order — feel the rhythm step up.

blocks.name: Cumulative — Learned Pool
blocks.description: Random rotation of 3, 4, 5 — adaptive engine prioritizes your weak speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: Five is roughly a 6-foot lag. Anchor it firmly before mixing.

tracks.title: Adding 6 MPH — Zone 1 Complete
tracks.objective: Complete the Touch zone with 6 MPH. All four Zone 1 speeds are now active and must be retained through the rest of the program.

blocks.name: Anchoring 6 MPH
focus: Full Zone 1 range now in play
blocks.description: 6 MPH completes the Touch zone. Roughly a 10-foot putt on slow greens or 8 feet on fast.

blocks.name: Sequence 3-4-5-6
blocks.description: Touch-zone sequence — train the schema across the full zone.

blocks.name: Cumulative — Touch Zone
blocks.description: Random Zone 1 rotation, weighted toward weak speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: Zone 1 is your foundation. Tomorrow's consolidation track refines all four speeds before the gate.

tracks.title: Zone 1 Consolidation
tracks.objective: Refine all four Touch-zone speeds without introducing anything new. The weakest speed gets the heaviest practice via the adaptive engine.

blocks.name: Weak-Speed Focus
focus: Block the same speed until it's firm
blocks.description: The adaptive engine selects your weakest Zone 1 speed and runs blocked practice on it.

blocks.name: Alternating Zone 1
blocks.description: Alternate across all four Touch speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: Goal: even accuracy across 3, 4, 5, and 6. If one speed is lagging, expect to see it more often.

tracks.title: Gate Test — Zone 1
tracks.objective: Zone 1 Gate Test. Passing unlocks 7 MPH and the Moderate zone.

blocks.name: Tune-Up Sequence
blocks.description: Light sequence on the gate speeds — rhythm check, not pressure.

blocks.name: Zone 1 Gate Test
blocks.description: Three putts each at 3, 4, 5, and 6 MPH. Pass: 8 of 12 in zone.

blocks.name: Cumulative — Touch Zone
blocks.description: Post-gate cumulative round to lock in the learned pool.

tracks.coachingNotes: Treat the gate like a single putt — focus only on the current speed.

tracks.title: Adding 7 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce the first Moderate speed. The Touch zone is fully retained via cumulative practice.

blocks.name: Warmup
blocks.description: Adaptive warmup across the Touch zone, ascending.

blocks.name: Anchoring 7 MPH
blocks.description: 7 MPH — first Moderate speed. Average green, 10-foot putt.

blocks.name: Bridge Sequence 5-6-7
blocks.description: Sequence the zone boundary — connect new 7 to known 5, 6.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 7
blocks.description: Random rotation across every speed learned so far. Weak speeds appear more often.

tracks.coachingNotes: Seven feels like a 10-foot putt on average green speed.

tracks.title: Adding 8 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce 8 MPH. The 7/8 boundary is one of the most important discriminations in real golf.

blocks.name: Anchoring 8 MPH
blocks.description: 8 MPH — the 7/8 discrimination is one of the most important in real golf.

blocks.name: Bridge Sequence 6-7-8
blocks.description: Step up through the zone boundary.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 8
blocks.description: Random rotation across all learned speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: A small grip pressure change is enough to swing between 7 and 8.

tracks.title: Adding 9 MPH — Zone 2 Complete
tracks.objective: Complete the Moderate zone with 9 MPH. All seven slow-and-medium speeds are now in rotation.

blocks.name: Anchoring 9 MPH
blocks.description: 9 MPH completes the Moderate zone — covers most real-round putts.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 9 (weighted 7-9)
blocks.description: Random rotation weighted toward the newly acquired Moderate speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: You now own the speed range that covers most putts on a real round.

tracks.title: Zone 2 Pressure Prep
tracks.objective: Condition the Moderate zone under pressure: make-in-a-row + ladder. Cumulative work keeps the Touch zone alive.

blocks.name: Make-In-A-Row — Moderate
blocks.description: Hit 3 consecutive in-zone putts at 7, 8, and 9. Reset on miss.

blocks.name: Ladder 7→9 then 9→7
blocks.description: Up the ladder then back down — six putts, no reset.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 9
blocks.description: Cumulative across the full learned pool.

tracks.coachingNotes: Pressure prep before a gate — slow your routine and stay committed.

tracks.title: Gate Test — Zone 2
tracks.objective: Zone 2 Gate Test. Passing unlocks 10 MPH and the Firm zone.

blocks.name: Tune-Up Sequence
blocks.description: Light sequence on the gate speeds — rhythm check.

blocks.name: Zone 2 Gate Test
blocks.description: Four putts each at 7, 8, 9 MPH. Pass: 8 of 12 in zone.

tracks.coachingNotes: Same routine as the gate — focus on the current speed.

tracks.title: Adding 10 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce the first Firm speed. From here on every new speed gets a dedicated consolidation track before the next is introduced — these speeds take longer to acquire.

blocks.name: Anchoring 10 MPH (heavy)
focus: Let stroke length do the work
blocks.description: 10 MPH — first Firm speed. Requires noticeable recruitment but tempo stays the same.

blocks.name: Anchoring 10 MPH (reinforcement)
blocks.description: Second pass on 10 to reinforce the motor pattern.

blocks.name: Bridge Sequence 8-9-10
blocks.description: Connect the new firm speed to the upper Moderate zone.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 10 (weighted 10)
blocks.description: Random rotation weighted toward the new speed.

tracks.coachingNotes: Ten requires noticeable recruitment. Don't let the stroke get long — let it accelerate.

tracks.title: 10 MPH Consolidation
tracks.objective: Lock in 10 MPH before adding 11. Variable work between 8-10 trains the upper Moderate / lower Firm boundary.

blocks.name: Variable 8-9-10
blocks.description: Train the boundary across three speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: Boundary work between zones is where real green-reading skill lives.

tracks.title: Adding 11 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce 11 MPH. Cumulative pool now spans 9 speeds.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 11 (weighted 11)
blocks.description: Full learned pool weighted to the new speed.

tracks.coachingNotes: Eleven and ten should feel distinctly different — small stroke change, big speed change.

tracks.title: 11 MPH Consolidation
tracks.objective: Refine 11 MPH and integrate 10/11. Cumulative random keeps the full learned pool fresh.
tracks.coachingNotes: Aim for parity at 10 and 11 by the end of this track.

tracks.title: Adding 12 MPH — Zone 3 Complete
tracks.objective: Complete the Firm zone with 12 MPH. Cumulative pool now spans 10 speeds — guarantee 2 reps each in the cumulative block.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 12 (weighted 12)
blocks.description: Full learned pool weighted to new speed.

tracks.coachingNotes: Twelve is firm but not maximal. Stay smooth.

tracks.title: 12 MPH Consolidation + Zone 3 Integration
tracks.objective: Lock in 12 MPH and integrate the full Firm zone. Cumulative practice weighted to 10-12.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 12 (weighted Firm)
blocks.description: Full pool, weighted to Firm zone.

blocks.name: Cool-Down
blocks.description: Easy adaptive close-out across Touch and Moderate zones.

tracks.coachingNotes: Last track before pressure prep. Make every Firm-zone putt count.

tracks.title: Pre-Gate 3 Pressure Prep
tracks.objective: Short, focused pressure session before Gate 3: one make-in-a-row on the weakest learned speed, one short ladder, then cool-down.

blocks.name: Make-In-A-Row — Weakest Speed
blocks.description: Adaptive engine selects your weakest learned speed. Hit 3 in a row in-zone. Reset on miss.

blocks.name: Pressure Ladder 3→9
blocks.description: Seven putts ascending through the slower half of the learned pool.

tracks.coachingNotes: Pressure should feel uncomfortable — that's the point. Tomorrow is the gate.

tracks.title: Gate Test — Zone 3
tracks.objective: Zone 3 Gate Test. Passing unlocks 13 MPH and the Power zone.

blocks.name: Zone 3 Gate Test
blocks.description: Four putts each at 10, 11, 12 MPH. Pass: 8 of 12 in zone.

tracks.coachingNotes: Routine before every putt — and especially before this one.

tracks.title: Adding 13 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce the first Power speed. The Power zone has the widest tolerance (±0.7) — but it's still the hardest zone to acquire.

blocks.name: Anchoring 13 MPH (heavy)
blocks.description: 13 MPH — first Power speed. Long lag putt. Tempo unchanged, stroke longer.

blocks.name: Bridge Sequence 11-12-13
blocks.description: Connect Power to upper Firm zone.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 13 (weighted 13)
blocks.description: Full learned pool weighted to new Power speed.

tracks.coachingNotes: Thirteen is a long lag. Don't over-recruit — let the stroke length do the work.

tracks.title: 13 MPH Consolidation
tracks.objective: Lock in 13 MPH. Variable work between 11-13 trains the Firm/Power boundary.
tracks.coachingNotes: Patience. The Power zone takes time. Cumulative work is doing real retention work in the background.

tracks.title: Adding 14 MPH
tracks.objective: Introduce 14 MPH. The cumulative block now hits the 80-putt cap and begins sampling rather than exhausting the learned pool.

blocks.name: Anchoring 14 MPH (heavy)
blocks.description: 14 MPH — second Power speed. Smooth tempo, full stroke.

blocks.name: Bridge Sequence 12-13-14
blocks.description: Step up through Firm into Power.

blocks.name: Cumulative — 3 to 14 (weighted 14, capped)
blocks.description: Capped at 24 putts; adaptive engine samples weak speeds first.

tracks.coachingNotes: Fourteen requires both length and acceleration. Smooth tempo first, force second.

tracks.title: 14 MPH Consolidation
tracks.objective: Refine 14 MPH and integrate 13/14. Cumulative pool samples weak speeds first.
tracks.coachingNotes: The boundary between 13 and 14 is where Power-zone touch lives.

tracks.title: Adding 15 MPH — Zone 4 Complete
tracks.objective: Complete the program's top speed. Every speed in the device's training range is now active.

blocks.name: Anchoring 15 MPH (heavy)
blocks.description: 15 MPH — top speed. Don't over-swing — let the device tell you when you've overshot.

tracks.coachingNotes: Fifteen is maximum. Don't over-swing — the device will tell you when you've overshot.

tracks.title: 15 MPH Consolidation + Zone 4 Integration
tracks.objective: Lock in 15 MPH and integrate the full Power zone.
tracks.coachingNotes: Last technical track before the gate. Use the cumulative block to identify and target weak speeds.

tracks.title: Pre-Gate 4 Pressure Prep
tracks.objective: Short, focused pressure session before Gate 4: one make-in-a-row on the weakest learned speed, one short ladder through Firm/Power, cool-down.

blocks.name: Make-In-A-Row — Weakest Speed
blocks.description: Adaptive engine selects weakest learned speed. Hit 3 in a row in-zone.

blocks.name: Pressure Ladder 9→15
blocks.description: Seven putts ascending through the faster half of the learned pool.

tracks.coachingNotes: Final gate prep. Stay process-focused.

tracks.title: Gate Test — Zone 4 + Phase Assessment
tracks.objective: Zone 4 Gate Test plus a multi-zone assessment. Passing unlocks Phase 4 — pure high-pressure transfer practice.

blocks.name: Zone 4 Gate Test
blocks.description: Four putts each at 13, 14, 15 MPH. Pass: 7 of 12 in zone.

blocks.name: Phase 3 Assessment — All Zones
blocks.description: Two-pass cumulative across the full 13-speed learned pool — measures retention before Phase 4.

tracks.coachingNotes: The assessment block samples every zone. Treat it like the Final dress rehearsal.

tracks.title: Random Under Pressure
tracks.objective: High contextual interference — random speed selection across the full range under a make-in-a-row pressure constraint, plus reactive practice.

blocks.name: Random Pressure (Make-3)
blocks.description: Random speed across full pool, but you must make 3 in-zone consecutively. Reset on miss.

blocks.name: Reactive Practice
blocks.description: Next target is selected based on your previous result — miss low and the next is at the same speed; hit and it changes.

blocks.name: Cumulative — Full Pool (capped)
blocks.description: Full random rotation across all 13 speeds.

tracks.coachingNotes: No new content from here on. Phase 4 trains transfer to real-round conditions.

tracks.title: Competition Simulation
tracks.objective: Two full rounds of the Combine game, back-to-back, with rest between. Trains performance recovery.

blocks.name: Combine — Round 1
blocks.description: Full Combine round across the 3–15 MPH target set. Score every shot.

blocks.name: Combine — Round 2
blocks.description: Second round, back-to-back. Trains performance recovery between rounds.

tracks.coachingNotes: Score every shot. Forget bad shots immediately and reset before the next.

tracks.title: Final Mastery Gate
tracks.objective: Final Mastery Gate Test. Sample every zone. Pass to complete the program.

blocks.name: Warmup
blocks.description: Final adaptive warmup across the full 13-speed learned pool.

blocks.name: Final Mastery Gate Test
blocks.description: Sample every zone (4 putts per zone, 2 speeds × 2 putts). Pass: 11 of 16 in zone.

blocks.name: Celebration
blocks.description: You completed every speed in the 3–15 MPH range. Free practice — no scoring.

tracks.coachingNotes: You've practiced every speed in every track for 30 sessions. Trust what you've built.

scientificFoundation.summary: Every track contains putts at every speed learned to date. Once introduced, a speed is revisited every subsequent track to prevent motor decay.
scientificFoundation.citation: Schmidt & Lee — Motor Control and Learning

scientificFoundation.summary: Practice difficulty is matched to current proficiency: blocked practice when a speed is new (low CI), random practice across the learned pool once acquired (high CI for retention and transfer).
Random practice across the full learned pool hurts in-session performance but improves retention and transfer to novel situations.
Variable practice across the full speed range builds one generalized putting motor schema rather than 13 discrete speed-specific patterns.
Make-in-a-row and ladder pressure blocks before each gate condition the user to perform under stress, training transfer from practice context to performance context.
